title: blob upsert does not validate content_type length — attacker can store arbitrary-length string in DB
description: jmap.rs handle_jmap_upload: content_type is extracted from the Content-Type header with no length cap. store.rs upsert_blob writes it directly to the blob table. An authenticated client can supply a Content-Type header of arbitrary length (axum default header limits do not prevent multi-KB values). This stores an unbounded string in SQLite and returns it verbatim in subsequent download responses. Scenario: authenticated client sends POST /jmap/upload with Content-Type: <64KB string>; string stored in DB, returned in download response headers.
